refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational policies, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a versioned history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked specificity on traceability, history management, and safety-critical aspects. Refinement adds: (1) explicit linkage to worksite mission context and safety policies, (2) baseline recording with traceability, (3) versioned history with audit trail, (4) safety margin derivation as a distinct performance target. This aligns with acceptance criteria and high safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance targets are automatically derived from documented worksite inputs (mission, policies, constraints), (b) each target is traceable to source worksite data, (c) baseline snapshots are recorded with timestamp and operator identity, (d) all updates are logged with reason and approval chain, (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and parse worksite mission context (site layout, task sequence, environmental constraints, operational policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using documented algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record performance baseline with full traceability to source worksite inputs and timestamp.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain versioned history of all performance requirement updates with audit trail (who, when, why, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Validate derived performance targets against worksite safety plan and operational policies before activation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission data, applies requirement derivation algorithms, records baselines to a versioned database, and maintains audit logs. Interfaces with Telemetry (inbound mission context) and Safety Services (outbound safety margin validation).

DP-B: Distributed Derivation – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules independently, with periodic synchronization to a central baseline store. Higher diffusion, lower cohesion.

DP-C: Manual Derivation with Template Library – Site Manager manually selects performance templates from a library, with system recording and versioning. Lower automation, higher human error risk, unsuitable for high safety criticality.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation Engine – Centralized engine for baseline derivation and validation, with local caching in Vehicle and Teleoperation for real-time performance monitoring. Balances centralization with operational responsiveness.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with integrated audit logging and safety validation.

potential_sources: Coupling to Telemetry interface (mission context inbound) is necessary and well-defined.
Coupling to Safety Services (safety margin validation outbound) is necessary and traceable to high safety criticality.
No unnecessary coupling to Vehicle or Teleoperation internals; interfaces are clean and asynchronous.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling to other system functions. The derivation engine is logically independent; it consumes standardized Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces. Audit trail and versioning are internal to the engine and do not create hidden dependencies.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, parseable format (e.g., JSON, XML) via Telemetry interface.
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be documented and validated.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or malformed, derived targets may be invalid. Mitigation: Implement validation gates and fallback to previous baseline.
RISK: Audit trail storage may grow unbounded. Mitigation: Implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: Safety margin derivation requires domain expertise. Mitigation: Algorithms must be co-developed with Safety Manager and validated against historical data.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit specification of: (1) derivation algorithm documentation and validation protocol, (2) audit trail schema and retention policy, (3) fallback behavior if mission context is unavailable or invalid, (4) interface contract for Telemetry (mission context schema) and SafetyAlert (safety margin validation response).

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Safety Services module shall own the Performance Derivation Engine, including: (a) ingestion of worksite mission context via Telemetry interface, (b) execution of performance requirement derivation algorithms, (c) recording and versioning of performance baselines, (d) maintenance of audit trail, (e) validation of derived targets against safety policies, (f) exposure of derived performance targets via SafetyAlert interface for consumption by Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Worksite mission context (site layout, task sequence, environmental constraints, operational policies, current equipment state). Schema: structured mission descriptor with task list, constraints, and policy references.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outbound: Derived performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) with traceability metadata (source worksite inputs, baseline version, timestamp, approval chain). Also outbound: validation status and any policy violations detected.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound: Operator requests to update or recalibrate performance baselines. Triggers re-derivation and audit log entry.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the top-level system container. Assigning IM to NGHE Solution would be too coarse; it would not establish clear ownership or interface boundaries. Diffusion risk is high because the derivation engine would be buried in the system-level abstraction.

Vehicle module is responsible for equipment operation and control. Performance derivation is a system-level concern, not vehicle-specific. Assigning to Vehicle would couple derivation logic to vehicle internals and create diffusion across multiple vehicle instances. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module handles remote operation and monitoring. Performance derivation is orthogonal to teleoperation control. Assigning to Teleoperation would create unnecessary coupling and diffusion across teleoperation instances. Not a good fit.

Safety Services is the natural home for performance requirement derivation because: (1) performance targets directly inform safety margins and operational limits, (2) safety validation is a core Safety Services responsibility, (3) audit trail and compliance logging align with safety governance, (4) centralized ownership ensures consistency and traceability, (5) low diffusion: single module boundary, clear interfaces. Excellent fit.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services is the optimal IM boundary because performance requirement derivation is fundamentally a safety and compliance function. The engine must validate derived targets against safety policies, maintain audit trails for regulatory compliance, and ensure traceability. Centralizing in Safety Services prevents diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation and ensures single source of truth for performance baselines.
containment_risks: RISK: If Safety Services becomes overloaded with non-safety functions, the module may lose cohesion. Mitigation: Establish clear scope boundary; performance derivation is safety-critical and belongs in Safety Services.

RISK: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may need to cache or replicate performance targets for real-time responsiveness. Mitigation: Define caching policy and invalidation protocol via SafetyAlert interface.

RISK: Tight coupling between Safety Services and Telemetry interface may create bottleneck if mission context updates are frequent. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous message queue and batch processing.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assignment to Safety Services is sound. Recommend: (1) define explicit interface contracts for Telemetry (mission context schema) and SafetyAlert (performance target schema), (2) establish caching and invalidation protocol for Vehicle and Teleoperation, (3) document audit trail schema and retention policy, (4) specify fallback behavior if Safety Services is unavailable.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly and sufficiently addresses CR-NGHE-005 Refined. The DP specifies: (1) automatic derivation from worksite mission context, (2) baseline recording with traceability, (3) versioned history with audit trail, (4) safety margin validation. No gaps or misalignments detected. DP is implementable and testable.

diffusion_risks: LOW RISK: Centralized engine in Safety Services minimizes diffusion. Single module boundary, clear interfaces.
LOW RISK: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces are standardized and reusable; no custom interfaces required.
MITIGATED RISK: Vehicle and Teleoperation may cache performance targets; caching policy and invalidation protocol must be documented.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services containment score is 0.9 (excellent). The module is cohesive, focused on safety and compliance, and naturally owns performance requirement derivation. Diffusion score is 0.1 (minimal). No evidence of diffusion across multiple modules or duplication of logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is verifiable. Verification can confirm: (a) performance targets are derived from documented worksite inputs, (b) traceability is maintained, (c) baselines are recorded with audit trail, (d) history is versioned and accessible. Test cases can be designed to validate derivation algorithms and audit logging.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: HIGH: Allocation of CR-NGHE-005 to Safety Services is clear and unambiguous. Ownership boundary is well-defined. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) are allocated to Safety Services with clear interface dependencies on Telemetry and Tasking (inbound) and SafetyAlert (outbound).

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Formalize interface contracts for Telemetry (mission context schema), SafetyAlert (performance target schema), and Tasking (baseline update request schema). Include examples and validation rules.
REVISION 2: Document performance derivation algorithms with mathematical specifications, validation protocol, and traceability to worksite mission context.
REVISION 3: Specify audit trail schema, retention policy, and archival strategy. Define who can access audit logs and under what conditions.
REVISION 4: Define fallback behavior if mission context is unavailable, incomplete, or invalid. Specify how Safety Services handles derivation failures.
REVISION 5: Establish caching and invalidation protocol for Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to ensure they have current performance targets without creating tight coupling.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5). Each sub-FR addresses a distinct functional concern: mission context ingestion, target derivation, baseline recording, history management, and validation. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to allocate each sub-FR to specific design parameters and implementation modules. The current level has established the top-level DP (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) and IM (Safety Services), but detailed design and implementation require further refinement.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest and parse worksite mission context (site layout, task sequence, environmental constraints, operational policies).
CR-NGHE-005.2: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context using documented algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record performance baseline with full traceability to source worksite inputs and timestamp.
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain versioned history of all performance requirement updates with audit trail (who, when, why, approval).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Validate derived performance targets against worksite safety plan and operational policies before activation.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, parseable format via Telemetry interface.
Performance derivation algorithms are deterministic and can be documented and validated.
Safety Services module has capacity and expertise to own performance derivation engine.
Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can consume performance targets via SafetyAlert interface without tight coupling.
Audit trail and versioning requirements are compatible with Safety Services' existing compliance infrastructure.
open_risks: RISK: Mission context may be incomplete or malformed, leading to invalid derived targets. Mitigation: Implement validation gates and fallback to previous baseline.
RISK: Audit trail storage may grow unbounded. Mitigation: Implement retention policy and archival strategy.
RISK: Safety margin derivation requires domain expertise. Mitigation: Algorithms must be co-developed with Safety Manager and validated against historical data.
RISK: Performance derivation engine may become a bottleneck if mission context updates are frequent. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous message queue and batch processing.
RISK: Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cache stale performance targets. Mitigation: Define invalidation protocol and refresh frequency.
evidence_needed: Worksite mission context schema and examples (from Site Manager and Operator).
Performance derivation algorithms with mathematical specifications and validation data (from System Engineer and Safety Manager).
Audit trail requirements and compliance standards (from Maintenance/Quality and Safety Manager).
Performance target consumption patterns in Vehicle and Teleoperation (from System Engineer).
Safety Services module capacity and existing compliance infrastructure (from System Engineer and Project Manager).
